title: Sweet Chewy Algerian Dumplings Tagine
description: A sweet, chewy plantbased tagine featuring tomato juice, dried fruit dumplings, and aromatic spices-ideal for a hangover cure.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine 300 ml warm water with 1 tsp instant yeast. Let sit 5–10 min until foamy.
In a large bowl, whisk together 200 g fine semolina, 100 g all‑purpose flour, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1 tsp mixed spice blend.
Add the activated yeast mixture, 2 tbsp olive oil, and stir to form a shaggy dough.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead 5–7 min until smooth and elastic; cover and let rise 1 h or until doubled.
Punch down the dough, divide into 1‑tbsp portions, roll into small discs (≈2 cm) and set aside on parchment.
In a medium pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil. Add 400 ml tomato juice, 1 tsp citrus zest blend, pinch of black pepper, and stir.
Fold in 250 g dried fruit mix and 1 tbsp honey; simmer gently 10 min until fruit plumps.
Gently drop the dumplings into the sauce, cover, and simmer 20–25 min on low heat until the dough is cooked through and chewy.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed; remove from heat.
Ladle the tagine into bowls and serve hot—ideal for a comforting, hangover‑cure dessert.
